prompt: Swyx telephony system analysis. We have a .NET 8 bridge (SwyxMessenger.exe) that attaches to CLMgr.exe COM session via Auto-Attach (DispIsLoggedIn=1). Dial/hangup via DispSimpleDialEx3/DispHookOn work correctly and we see line state changes. BUT when we dial, the original SwyxIt!.exe client pops up its GUI window. Questions: (1) What mechanism in CLMgr or Swyx Server causes SwyxIt!.exe to launch/show when a call is made? (2) The registry key EnablePowerDialMode (DWORD, 0=standard, 1=power dial/headless mode) exists in docs but is NOT set on this system. Could setting it to 1 prevent the popup? (3) SwyxIt! seems to establish the SIP/server connection during its startup. If true, what specific COM methods or protocols does it use that CLMgr alone does not? (4) Is there a way to run fully headless (no SwyxIt!) while retaining full call control, audio, and presence? Consider: CLMgr v14.25, SwyxIt v14.10, .NET 8 x86 bridge, RegisterUserEx with ctiMaster parameter. Give concrete actionable answers.
